methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mazama is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Sriram Vasantharajan that has raised $30M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—alongside a broader universe of 8 private-sector climate tech companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $30M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies $60M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 direct comparables yields $150M, with the full range spanning $110M to $200M. These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $51M (low), $105M (median), and $172M (high). The median reflects equal weighting of the two approaches.

The primary uncertainty lies in calibrating Mazama's position within the peer distribution. The stage-progression method assumes minimal capital efficiency premium relative to peers; the peer-median method anchors to observable transactions but assumes Mazama's competitive and market positioning is representative of the sample. Validation would require deeper examination of unit economics, market timing, and technology differentiation relative to Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy. The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M–$172M, with $105M as the central estimate.
